item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
item.description: A federal judge in Tennessee dismissed the criminal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Friday, finding that the investigation into his alleged involvement with human trafficking was “tainted.”

media:content.media:text: Kilmar Abrego Garcia arrives with his wife, Jennifer Vasquez Sura, at U.S. District Court for the District of Maryland on Dec. 22, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia returned to the U.S. in June 2025 after being deported to El Salvador.

item.title: Judge dismisses charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia in human trafficking case

item.description: The Trump administration announced that people with temporary visas seeking to adjust their immigration status to obtain green cards must return to their home countries to “do so through consular processing,” in a major change from current practice. NBC News' Jonathan Allen explains the impact and how the new requirement will be enforced.

media:content.media:title: DHS to require green card applicants to return to home countries to apply
media:content.media:description: The Trump administration announced that people with temporary visas seeking to adjust their immigration status to obtain green cards must return to their home countries to “do so through consular processing,” in a major change from current practice. NBC News' Jonathan Allen explains the impact and how the new requirement will be enforced.

item.title: DHS to require green card applicants to return to home countries to apply

item.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard announced her resignation from President Trump’s Cabinet. Her last day will be June 30th.

media:content.media:title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as director of national intelligence
media:content.media:description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard announced her resignation from President Trump’s Cabinet. Her last day will be June 30th.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as director of national intelligence

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: Rapper Rob Base died Friday at age 59 after a cancer diagnosis, according to a post on his social media.

media:content.media:text: Rob Base lying down on a white seamless backdrop.
media:content.media:description: Rapper Rob Base, aka Robert Ginyard, in New York in 1989.

item.title: ‘It Takes Two’ rapper Rob Base dies at 59

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
item.description: An estimated 40,000 people in Orange County, California, have been ordered to evacuate the area over fears that a tank containing a toxic chemical may explode, officials said Friday

media:content.media:text: Southern California Chemical Tank
media:content.media:description: Water is sprayed on a tank that overheated at an aerospace plant in Garden Grove, Calif., on Friday.

item.title: 40,000 Southern Californians ordered to evacuate amid threat of chemical explosion

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Megan Shannon
item.description: President Donald Trump defended his economic record and praised Republican Rep. Mike Lawler, who is seeking re-election in a very competitive New York district.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump arrives to speak at Rockland Community College in Suffern, N.Y., on May 22, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump praised vulnerable GOP Rep. Mike Lawler during an event at Rockland Community College in Suffern, N.Y., on May 22.

media:content.media:text: Rep. Michael Lawler, R-N.Y., arrives at Rockland Community College in Suffern, N.Y., on Friday, May 22, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump praised vulnerable GOP Rep. Mike Lawler during an event at Rockland Community College in Suffern, N.Y., on May 22.

item.title: Trump praises vulnerable GOP Rep. Mike Lawler as ‘fantastic’ during a joint rally in New York

item.dc:creator: Kyle Stewart
Jennifer Jett
item.description: House Republicans delayed a vote on a war powers resolution that would restrict President Donald Trump’s ability to continue U.S. military operations in Iran.

media:content.media:text: Iran-Music Ceremony On Ruins Of Destroyed Synagogue
media:content.media:description: An unidentified man places an Iranian national flag on the ruins of a synagogue in Tehran, Iran, on May 14, 2026.

item.title: House Republicans cancel vote curbing Trump on Iran

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: A new burst of diplomatic action intensified Thursday in a push to break the deadlock between the United States and Iran.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-IRAN-US-ISRAEL-WAR-PROTEST
media:content.media:description: Women hold Iran's national flag and photos of country's supreme leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei and his predecessor and late father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei during an anti-US and Israel protest at the Hafte Tir Square in Tehran on May 17, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Iran And US Remain In Stalemate Over Strait Of Hormuz
media:content.media:description: Nearly three months since the war began with U.S. and Israeli airstrikes Feb. 28, Iran maintains a chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz.

media:content.media:text: JD Vance arrives in Pakistan.
media:content.media:description: Munir welcomed Vice President JD Vance to Islamabad last month for talks that fell short of a peace deal.

item.title: Iran-U.S. diplomacy intensifies as Trump seeks ‘right answers,’ Tehran signals gaps ‘reduced’

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: Racing legend Kyle Busch was coughing up blood and lying on a bathroom floor when somebody called for help from a General Motors training facility in North Carolina, according to the 911 call obtained by NBC News.

media:content.media:text: Kyle Busch at Daytona International Speedway in 2024.
media:content.media:description: Kyle Busch at Daytona International Speedway in 2024.

item.title: 911 call reveals Kyle Busch was coughing up blood, short of breath day before death

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: Long before Tony Waller, an Assemblies of God pastor, was convicted of raping girls, children told church leaders about hidden cameras, drugged drinks and abuse, a lawsuit says.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy Stephanie Davis: Houston Cofield for NBC News
media:content.media:text: Stephanie Davis as a child, wearing the sash from the Assemblies of God's Missionettes girls program; Refuge Church of The Assemblies of God in Jonesboro, Ark.
media:content.media:description: Stephanie Davis as a child, wearing the sash from the Assemblies of God's Missionettes girls program; Refuge Church of The Assemblies of God in Jonesboro, Ark.

media:content.media:text: Tony Waller in court in Jonesboro, Ark., May 2015
media:content.media:description: Tony Waller was sentenced to life in prison in 2016.

media:content.media:text: "Unfortunately, nobody else had the courage to put their foot down,” Stephanie Davis said of failures to remove Tony Waller from ministry.
media:content.media:description: "Unfortunately, nobody else had the courage to put their foot down,” Stephanie Davis said of failures to remove Tony Waller from ministry.

media:content.media:text: The Assemblies of God headquarters in Springfield, Mo.
media:content.media:description: The Assemblies of God promotes child safety guidelines but has resisted making them mandatory.

media:content.media:text: Stephanie Davis stands outside the Refuge Church of the Assemblies of God
media:content.media:description: Five other women have signed on to Stephanie Davis' lawsuit.

item.title: Arkansas pastor abused girls for 15 years without church stopping him, lawsuit alleges

item.description: Watch highlights of the star-studded sendoff as Stephen Colbert brings the curtain down on 33 years of “The Late Show,” with legends like Jon Stewart, Paul McCartney, Bryan Cranston, and many surprises.

media:content.media:title: Watch highlights from Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' finale
media:content.media:description: Watch highlights of the star-studded sendoff as Stephen Colbert brings the curtain down on 33 years of “The Late Show,” with legends like Jon Stewart, Paul McCartney, Bryan Cranston, and many surprises.

item.title: Watch highlights from Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' finale

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Shop the best Memorial Day deals on mattresses, home appliances, beauty, tech and more from brands like Brooklinen, Ninja, Levoit, EltaMD and Apple.

media:content.media:credit: Amazon; Kara Birnbaum / NBC News; Brooklinen
media:content.media:text: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.
media:content.media:description: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.

media:content.media:text: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.
media:content.media:description: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.

item.title: The 93+ absolute best Memorial Day sales worth shopping

item.description: Watch live coverage as SpaceX launches the twelfth test flight of Starship. The company claims the third-generation prototype, dubbed V3, is larger, more powerful and a step closer to being fully reusable. The rocket is scheduled to liftoff from SpaceX's StarBase in Brownsville, Texas.

media:content.media:title: SpaceX launches prototype of Starship rocket in crucial test flight
media:content.media:description: Watch live coverage as SpaceX launches the twelfth test flight of Starship. The company claims the third-generation prototype, dubbed V3, is larger, more powerful and a step closer to being fully reusable. The rocket is scheduled to liftoff from SpaceX's StarBase in Brownsville, Texas.

item.title: SpaceX launches prototype of Starship rocket in crucial test flight

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: An elderly California couple found dead in a suspected murder-suicide had recently been scammed by someone posing as actor Tom Selleck, a longtime friend said

media:content.media:text: Donald Whitaker, 80, and his wife Karen Whitaker,
media:content.media:description: Donald and Karen Whitaker.

item.title: California woman dead in murder-suicide may have given thousands to a scammer claiming to be Tom Selleck

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
item.description: An estimated 40,000 people in Orange County, California, have been ordered to evacuate the area over fears that a tank containing a toxic chemical may explode, officials said Friday

media:content.media:text: Southern California Chemical Tank
media:content.media:description: Water is sprayed on a tank that overheated at an aerospace plant in Garden Grove, Calif., on Friday.

item.title: 40,000 Southern Californians ordered to evacuate amid threat of chemical explosion

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: Rapper Rob Base died Friday at age 59 after a cancer diagnosis, according to a post on his social media.

media:content.media:text: Rob Base lying down on a white seamless backdrop.
media:content.media:description: Rapper Rob Base, aka Robert Ginyard, in New York in 1989.

item.title: ‘It Takes Two’ rapper Rob Base dies at 59

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
Madeline Morrison
item.description: First responders were exposed to fentanyl and sickened after arriving at a rural New Mexico home earlier this week to investigate a possible overdose that left three people dead, officials said Friday.

media:content.media:text: New Mexico State Police are investigating after 18 first responders were hospitalized after being exposed to a substance at a home where three people died Wednesday in the small town of Mountainair. (KOB)
media:content.media:description: Emergency services near the hazmat scene in New Mexico on Wednesday.

item.title: First responders exposed to fentanyl in deadly New Mexico incident, officials say

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Megan Shannon
item.description: President Donald Trump defended his economic record and praised Republican Rep. Mike Lawler, who is seeking re-election in a very competitive New York district.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump arrives to speak at Rockland Community College in Suffern, N.Y., on May 22, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump praised vulnerable GOP Rep. Mike Lawler during an event at Rockland Community College in Suffern, N.Y., on May 22.

media:content.media:text: Rep. Michael Lawler, R-N.Y., arrives at Rockland Community College in Suffern, N.Y., on Friday, May 22, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump praised vulnerable GOP Rep. Mike Lawler during an event at Rockland Community College in Suffern, N.Y., on May 22.

item.title: Trump praises vulnerable GOP Rep. Mike Lawler as ‘fantastic’ during a joint rally in New York

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: President Donald Trump has reinforced his sway with Republican primary voters. But that success has not translated to the halls of the Capitol.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump, wearing a white USA ballcap, points with one finger.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump gestures as he speaks to reporters on the South Lawn of the White House on May 15, 2026.

item.title: As Trump flexes his muscle on the campaign trail, he faces rare setbacks on Capitol Hill: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Berkeley Lovelace Jr.
item.description: No child deaths have been definitively linked to Covid vaccines, according to a report from the Food and Drug Administration that was quietly made public last week.

media:content.media:text: Food And Drug Administration Headquarters In Maryland
media:content.media:description: The FDA analysis was dated Dec. 5 but was not made public until this month.

item.title: No child deaths definitively linked to covid shots, FDA says

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
item.description: A federal judge in Tennessee dismissed the criminal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Friday, finding that the investigation into his alleged involvement with human trafficking was “tainted.”

media:content.media:text: Kilmar Abrego Garcia arrives with his wife, Jennifer Vasquez Sura, at U.S. District Court for the District of Maryland on Dec. 22, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia returned to the U.S. in June 2025 after being deported to El Salvador.

item.title: Judge dismisses charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia in human trafficking case

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Kettle Cuisine, based in Lynn, Massachusetts, has recalled its minestrone sold at Whole Foods Market locations nationwide, affecting products distributed across 17 states and the District of Columbia

media:content.media:text: Image:
media:content.media:description: Shoppers depart a Whole Foods Market.

media:content.media:text: Whole Foods Soup recall
media:content.media:description: Shoppers depart a Whole Foods Market.

item.title: Minestrone soup served at Whole Foods Market is recalled

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Joe Prince-Wright, NBC Sports
item.description: The New York Knicks beat the Cleveland Cavaliers convincingly last night and are now two wins away from their first NBA Finals since 1999.

media:content.media:text: Josh Hart.
media:content.media:description: New York Knicks guard Josh Hart drives to the basket against Cleveland Cavaliers center Jarrett Allen during the first quarter of Game 2 of the Eastern Conference finals of the 2026 NBA playoffs at Madison Square Garden.

media:content.media:text: James Harden.
media:content.media:description: New York Knicks guard Josh Hart drives to the basket against Cleveland Cavaliers center Jarrett Allen during the first quarter of Game 2 of the Eastern Conference finals of the 2026 NBA playoffs at Madison Square Garden.

media:content.media:text: Duckens Nazon.
media:content.media:description: New York Knicks guard Josh Hart drives to the basket against Cleveland Cavaliers center Jarrett Allen during the first quarter of Game 2 of the Eastern Conference finals of the 2026 NBA playoffs at Madison Square Garden.

media:content.media:text: Eli Junior Kroupi.
media:content.media:description: New York Knicks guard Josh Hart drives to the basket against Cleveland Cavaliers center Jarrett Allen during the first quarter of Game 2 of the Eastern Conference finals of the 2026 NBA playoffs at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Knicks beat down Cavs, pull within two wins of NBA Finals

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: Jan. 6 prosecutor, Trump administration targets sue over ‘weaponization’ fund

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump.
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump on May 21 in the Oval Office at the White House.

item.title: Jan. 6 prosecutor, Trump administration targets sue over ‘weaponization’ fund

item.dc:creator: Lauren Dunn
item.description: One of the first cases of Ebola that Rose Tchwenko heard about in the current outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo was in a healthcare worker.

media:content.media:text: Relatives of Ebola victims sit outside the General Referral Hospital.
media:content.media:description: Relatives of Ebola victims sit outside the General Referral Hospital on Thursday in Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo.

media:content.media:text: Health Officials Race To Contain Deadly Ebola Outbreak In Congo
media:content.media:description: Relatives of Ebola victims sit outside the General Referral Hospital on Thursday in Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo.

media:content.media:text: Health Officials Race To Contain Deadly Ebola Outbreak In Congo
media:content.media:description: Relatives of Ebola victims sit outside the General Referral Hospital on Thursday in Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo.

media:content.media:text: DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: Relatives of Ebola victims sit outside the General Referral Hospital on Thursday in Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: Relatives of Ebola victims sit outside the General Referral Hospital on Thursday in Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo.

item.title: Misinfo, strained resources and armed conflict hamper Ebola response
